checks.finding: All 13 requirements are observable: REQ-002 tool response fields are enumerated field-by-field (e.g. list_workflow_runs returns id/name/status/conclusion/branch/event/created/updated/run number/html_url), REQ-003/REQ-006 write-exclusion and error-mapping are deterministic and machine-checkable via static grep (AC-007) and fake-HTTP integration tests (AC-010), REQ-008's 256 KiB truncation with truncated flag and returnedBytes is a concrete measurable threshold, REQ-009 dist-parity and REQ-010/011 installer behavior are verifiable via existing harnesses. No in-scope requirement relies on unmeasurable intent language.

Each Goal in requirements.md maps to acceptance criteria: read-only CI info retrieval -> AC-001..AC-005/AC-015; zero-write safety -> AC-006/AC-007; token non-leakage -> AC-008/AC-009; upstream error/rate-limit normalization -> AC-010/AC-011; installer distribution/idempotent registration -> AC-016/AC-017/AC-018. No stated goal lacks a corresponding AC row.

Acceptance criteria describe concrete externally observable outputs: exact response fields (AC-001..AC-005), exact error codes and details.status values per branch (AC-010), exact truncation flags and byte counts (AC-004), grep-detectable absence of write methods/child_process/eval (AC-007), and installer file-placement/registration behavior under isolated HOME (AC-016..AC-018). None rely on vague intent phrasing.

Non-goals section explicitly excludes GitHub write operations, artifact binary download (deferred to OQ-002), non-Actions GitHub features, GitHub Enterprise Server base-URL support (deferred to OQ-003), gh CLI token exec, and knowledge-mcp/repo-mirror. Boundaries are explicit rather than left to expand implicitly.

Material constraints are explicit: read-only PAT via env var only with scrubbing requirement (REQ-005), GET-only HTTP method enforcement (REQ-003), 256 KiB log truncation with tail-priority retention (REQ-008), Node.js >=20 runtime requirement and ADR-0003 bundling (REQ-009), Security Boundaries table defining trust boundaries B1-B3 with auth/authz and data classification, and Assumptions section stating read-only PAT provisioning and platform support. Open product decisions (OQ-001 repo resolution precedence, OQ-004 token env var name) are flagged as open questions with owners and resolution paths rather than silently assumed, which is appropriate at Phase 1 rather than a specification gap.

The Risks section identifies three high-risk claims (write-tool/write-API leakage, token leakage, upstream API/rate-limit variance, and log-size memory exhaustion) and each has a stated validation surface: write-leakage -> static GET-only + write-method-absence checks and negative tests (AC-006/AC-007); token leakage -> mandatory canary scrubbing test (AC-009); upstream variance -> fake-HTTP error-path tests covering all branches including headerless-403 (AC-010/AC-011); log memory exhaustion -> streaming + 256 KiB tail-priority cap (REQ-008, AC-004). No high-risk claim is left without a planned validation surface.
